channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news aggregator covering conflicts, diplomacy, and global affairs

item.title: 🇦🇫🇷🇺 Taliban signs weapons deal with Russia, warns Pakistan
item.description: Afghanistan's Taliban regime signed a military-technical cooperation agreement with Russia on May 27, focusing on repairing Russian-made equipment. Taliban defense minister warned Pakistan would "soon no longer dare" to attack Afghan territory.
item.dc:subject: Defense Industry

item.title: 🛢️📉 US crude inventories in freefall, down 8 million barrels
item.description: US commercial crude stockpiles fell 8 million barrels to 433.7 million, now 3% below the five-year average. Brent rose to .24, WTI to .99 as supply tightens amid Middle East crisis.
item.dc:subject: Energy

item.title: 🇦🇪💻 UAE sees opportunity in Hormuz crisis to protect global data
item.description: As Iran threatens subsea cables through the Strait of Hormuz, the UAE is positioning itself to become a critical hub for data security. Over 95% of intercontinental data traffic travels through subsea cables.
item.dc:subject: Geopolitics

item.title: 🇯🇵🇨🇳 Can Japan's Takaichi repair fractured China ties?
item.description: Japan-China relations are at their most difficult point in years. Despite PM Takaichi's hawkish reputation, analysts recall how Abe Shinzo—once viewed similarly—ultimately stabilized relations with Beijing.
item.dc:subject: Asia-Pacific

item.title: ⚔️🇺🇸 US delaying weapons deliveries to allies, raising reliability concerns
item.description: Multiple US allies have been told their weapons deliveries are being slowed as Washington prioritizes replenishing its own stockpiles. The move has sent shockwaves through European and Asian defense communities.
item.dc:subject: Defense Industry

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drones Strike St. Petersburg Oil Terminal as Zelenskyy Vows Escalation
item.description: Ukrainian long-range drones hit an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, setting it ablaze just days before Putin's economic forum. The attack came as Russia launched its largest drone barrage on Ukraine in months, killing 23 civilians.
item.dc:subject: Ukraine-Russia War

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iranian Drone Attack on Kuwait Airport Kills One, Tests Fragile Ceasefire
item.description: Iranian drones heavily damaged Kuwait International Airport's passenger terminal, killing an Indian national and wounding 63. Kuwait expelled two Iranian diplomats as the four-month-old US-Iran ceasefire showed increasing strain.
item.dc:subject: Middle East

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Acknowledges Calling Netanyahu "Crazy" as Israel-Hezbollah Fighting Complicates Iran Talks
item.description: President Trump confirmed he called Netanyahu "crazy" in a phone call, expressing frustration that Israel's ongoing conflict with Hezbollah is holding back peace negotiations with Iran. Both leaders insisted their relationship remains solid.
item.dc:subject: Diplomacy

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Support for Same-Sex Marriage Declines for First Time in Decades, Gallup Poll Shows
item.description: A new Gallup poll reveals that US support for same-sex marriage has flattened at 65%, down from 71% in 2022-2023. The decline is driven almost entirely by dropping acceptance among Republicans, now at 37%.
item.dc:subject: US Politics

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Cuts Domestic Fuel Prices as Iran War Impacts Energy Markets
item.description: China announced it will lower retail price caps on gasoline and diesel from June 5, marking the second cut since the Iran war began and reflecting constrained global energy supplies.
item.dc:subject: Energy

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Says Lebanon Operations to Continue Despite Ceasefire Announcement
item.description: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz stated that military operations in Lebanon will continue and troops will not withdraw, despite the announcement of a renewed ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:subject: Middle East

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan to Expand Anti-Ship Missile Arsenal to Counter China Threat
item.description: Taiwan plans to increase its anti-ship missile arsenal to over 1,800 by early 2029, enhancing capabilities to counter potential blockade or invasion threats from China.
item.dc:subject: US-China

item.title: 🇳🇿 China Bans Four New Zealand Lawmakers Over Taiwan Visit
item.description: Beijing barred four New Zealand MPs from entering China, Hong Kong, and Macau for one year as punishment for visiting Taiwan, prompting Wellington to express concern to Beijing.
item.dc:subject: Diplomacy

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Tells NATO Allies to Boost Air and Naval Forces as Washington Steps Back
item.description: A top US general said Washington expects European NATO allies and Canada to swiftly increase aircraft and naval contributions to alliance defense plans as the US reduces its role in these areas.
item.dc:subject: Europe

item.title: 🏛️ US House votes to curb Trump's Iran war powers in rebuke
item.description: The House passed a war powers resolution with a 215-208 vote, with four Republicans joining Democrats. The measure aims to limit presidential military authority over Iran.
item.dc:subject: US Politics

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU advances Ukraine and Moldova membership talks after Hungary drops veto
item.description: All 27 EU member states have backed opening formal accession negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova after Hungary reached an agreement with Kyiv, clearing a key hurdle for EU enlargement amid ongoing war.
item.dc:subject: Europe

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesia arrests deputy immigration minister in free meals program graft scandal
item.description: Indonesian authorities detained the deputy immigration minister on corruption charges, one day after arresting the former head of an agency overseeing President Prabowo Subianto's flagship free meals initiative.
item.dc:subject: Southeast Asia

item.title: 🇴🇲 Oman resists US pressure to break ties with Iran over Hormuz Strait
item.description: Oman is maintaining its diplomatic relationship with Iran despite US pressure to sever ties. The Gulf sultanate has historically served as a mediator between Iran and Western powers.
item.dc:subject: Diplomacy

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan faces plastic shortages as Iran war disrupts naphtha supply
item.description: Japan is experiencing shortages of plastic bags, trays and gloves as the Iran conflict cuts off naphtha supplies from the Middle East, the main source for Japanese petrochemical production.
item.dc:subject: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 🇨🇳🌍 Trump and Putin visits highlight China's central role in great-power diplomacy
item.description: Back-to-back summits with the US and Russian presidents underscore Beijing's growing ability to manage its two most important relationships on separate tracks, analysts say.
item.dc:subject: Diplomacy

item.title: 🇦🇫🇷🇺 Taliban and Russia deepen ties amid frayed Western relations
item.description: Tanzania's president visits Russia while the Taliban strengthens cooperation with Moscow, signaling shifting alliances as Western influence wanes in key regions.
item.dc:subject: Diplomacy

item.title: 🛢️🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia and Russia tighten grip on oil market as UAE exits OPEC
item.description: The UAE's departure from OPEC consolidates Saudi and Russian control over global oil markets, with implications for energy prices and geopolitical alignments.
item.dc:subject: Energy

item.title: 🇨🇳🇳🇿 China bans four New Zealand MPs for one year over Taiwan visit
item.description: Beijing sanctioned the lawmakers after they visited Taiwan last month, barring them from China for a year in the latest diplomatic row over the self-governed island.
item.dc:subject: US-China

item.title: 🇩🇪🇷🇺 Germany blames Russia after losing UN Security Council seat
item.description: Berlin accused Moscow of campaigning against its bid after Germany lost to Portugal and Austria for non-permanent seats. Johann Wadephul said Russia stirred opposition due to German support for Ukraine.
item.dc:subject: Europe

item.title: 🔍🇨🇳 Five Eyes nations warn China using LinkedIn to recruit Western spies
item.description: Intelligence agencies from US, UK, Canada, Australia and New Zealand issued rare joint warning that Beijing is using fake job profiles on professional networking sites to target security personnel with access to classified information.
item.dc:subject: Cyber
